description:
# .43.F — lower_case body REPLACED by matrix dispatch (mechanical)

## What changes

lower_case (src/ir_lower.rs ~2252): same shape as .D.1, simpler.

- case body is ALREADY always cont-fn-wrapped (no inline fast path
  to worry about — .D.0 doesn't apply here)
- Replace the per-clause try_blocks cascade with lower_pattern_matrix
- Body callback wraps each leaf in mint_cont_fn (today's pattern)

## What stays

- Subject lowering + parking
- join_opt for non-tail position
- Per-clause cont_fn (minted inside body callback at each leaf)

## What's removed

- ~50 LOC of per-clause try_blocks cascade
- One copy of the dispatch-by-cascade pattern (was three; .D.1 already
  removed one)

## Acceptance

- case-using fixtures: meaningfully tighter OR equivalent
- Three-path parity
- Existing lower_case unit tests still pass (semantic invariants)

## Risk

case currently errors on guards. v1 keeps that errors — matrix
encounters a guarded row, the body callback errors with the same
\"case guard (deferred)\" message. Future ticket: guard support.
title: fz-ul4.43.F: wire lower_case onto matrix
